You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From efb860b3ff8ebcf606199b8d0d432f76898040da Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date: Tue, 4 Sep 2018 18:19:32 +0000
Subject: [PATCH] When generating a redirect to a directory in the Default
 Servlet, avoid generating a protocol relative redirect.

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/tomcat/trunk@1840055 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
index c603b7f0cf..5527d05f4b 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
@@ -1313,6 +1313,10 @@ public class DefaultServlet extends HttpServlet {
             location.append('?');
             location.append(request.getQueryString());
         }
+        // Avoid protocol relative redirects
+        while (location.length() > 1 && location.charAt(1) == '/') {
+            location.deleteCharAt(0);
+        }
         response.sendRedirect(response.encodeRedirectURL(location.toString()));
     }
 

diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
index e7220b7969..f8734ab8aa 100644
--- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
+++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
@@ -72,6 +72,10 @@
       <fix>
         <bug>62667</bug>: Add recursion to rewrite substitution parsing. (remm)
       </fix>
+      <fix>
+        When generating a redirect to a directory in the Default Servlet, avoid
+        generating a protocol relative redirect. (markt)
+      </fix>
     </changelog>
   </subsection>
   <subsection name="Coyote">
-- 
2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **DefaultServlet.java**
   - Added a new block of code to prevent the generation of protocol-relative redirects when constructing the redirect location for directories. Specifically, it checks if the second character of the `location` string is a '/' and removes the first character if true. This ensures that the redirect URL is properly formatted.

2. **changelog.xml**
   - Updated the changelog to include a new entry that documents the fix made in the `DefaultServlet.java`. The entry describes the change as avoiding the generation of protocol-relative redirects when redirecting to a directory in the Default Servlet.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modification in `DefaultServlet.java` directly addresses a bug related to the generation of redirects, ensuring that the application behaves correctly by not producing protocol-relative URLs. The corresponding entry in `changelog.xml` serves to document this fix, providing context and traceability for future reference. This relationship highlights the importance of maintaining clear documentation alongside code changes to facilitate understanding and maintenance of the codebase.

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug.

file:<file name>

Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From efb860b3ff8ebcf606199b8d0d432f76898040da Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date: Tue, 4 Sep 2018 18:19:32 +0000
Subject: [PATCH] When generating a redirect to a directory in the Default
 Servlet, avoid generating a protocol relative redirect.

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/tomcat/trunk@1840055 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
index e7220b7969..f8734ab8aa 100644
--- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
+++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
@@ -72,6 +72,10 @@
       <fix>
         <bug>62667</bug>: Add recursion to rewrite substitution parsing. (remm)
       </fix>
+      <fix>
+        When generating a redirect to a directory in the Default Servlet, avoid
+        generating a protocol relative redirect. (markt)
+      </fix>
     </changelog>
   </subsection>
   <subsection name="Coyote">
-- 
2.34.1



diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
index c603b7f0cf..5527d05f4b 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/DefaultServlet.java
@@ -1313,6 +1313,10 @@ public class DefaultServlet extends HttpServlet {
             location.append('?');
             location.append(request.getQueryString());
         }
+        // Avoid protocol relative redirects
+        while (location.length() > 1 && location.charAt(1) == '/') {
+            location.deleteCharAt(0);
+        }
         response.sendRedirect(response.encodeRedirectURL(location.toString()));
     }

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`changelog.xml`**:
   - Added a new entry in the changelog to document a fix related to the Default Servlet. The entry states that when generating a redirect to a directory, the system will now avoid generating a protocol-relative redirect.

2. **`DefaultServlet.java`**:
   - Introduced a new code block that checks the `location` string. If the second character of the `location` is a '/', it removes the leading character. This modification ensures that protocol-relative redirects are avoided when generating the redirect URL.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modification in `DefaultServlet.java` directly implements the change described in the new entry added to `changelog.xml`. The code change addresses the issue of generating protocol-relative redirects, which is documented in the changelog as a fix. This relationship highlights the connection between the code implementation and its documentation, ensuring that users are informed of the specific behavior change in the Default Servlet's redirect functionality.

You are a programming expert.

Reason: The provided code snippet constructs a redirect URL by appending a forward slash to the request URI without checking if the request URI already contains a leading slash. This can lead to the generation of a protocol-relative URL, which is the root cause of the bug described. Therefore, the code snippet is indeed buggy as it does not implement the fix that checks for and removes leading double slashes.

Result: yes

Result: no
